intro: Kohleria warszewiczii, also known as Warszewicz's kohleria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical rainforests of Central and South America. Traditionally, Kohleria warszewiczii has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kohleria warszewiczii include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and iridoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities. In herbal medicine, Kohleria warszewiczii is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
